books.title: Monsieur Lecoq
books.author: Émile Gaboriau
books.summary: Monsieur Lecoq’s first case in which he tries to determine the identity of a man in custody for three murders.

books.title: Seven Pillars of Wisdom
books.author: T. E. Lawrence
books.summary: T. E. Lawrence’s memoir of leading the Arab revolt against the Ottoman empire during World War I.

books.title: Memoirs of a Foxhunting Man
books.author: Siegfried Sassoon
books.summary: A young boy comes of age in the idyllic English countryside just before the Great War tears Europe apart.

books.title: Recollections of Full Years
books.author: Helen Herron Taft
books.summary: Helen Herron Taft, wife of William Howard Taft, the twenty-seventh President of the United States, recalls her life married to one of America’s most influential political figures.

books.title: The Enchanted Castle
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Three children discover a wonderful garden and castle, and come into possession of a magic ring which entangles them in many strange adventures.

books.title: Tartuffe
books.author: Molière
books.summary: A father announces his daughter will marry Tartuffe, a sanctimonious fraud and hypocrite.

books.title: Edward III
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: Edward III declares himself the true heir of France, which launches the Hundred Years’ War.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: E. M. Forster
books.summary: A collection of E. M. Forster’s short stories, ordered by date of their original publication.

books.title: Sinister Street
books.author: Compton Mackenzie
books.summary: A precocious boy grows to adulthood while struggling with both his own and society’s expectations of his role in life.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Manly Wade Wellman
books.summary: A collection of horror and science fiction stories by Manly Wade Wellman.

books.title: The Education of Henry Adams
books.author: Henry Adams
books.summary: A prominent and caustic historian reflects on the reasons that “the more he was educated, the less he understood,” and what he believes the future holds for American society.

books.title: The Eye of Osiris
books.author: R. Austin Freeman
books.summary: A newly qualified doctor helps his former teacher, a famed forensic scientist, unravel a case involving ancient Egyptian artifacts, a missing person, and a mysterious will.

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W. E. B. Du Bois
books.summary: A seminal collection of essays on the African-American experience.

books.title: Democracy and Education
books.author: John Dewey
books.summary: A critical survey of the philosophy and aims of education in a democratic society.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Mina Loy
books.summary: A collection of poems by Mina Loy.

books.title: The Devil’s Dictionary
books.author: Ambrose Bierce
books.summary: A witty, sharp collection of sardonic word definitions from the 1800s and early 1900s that provide a snapshot into the irreverent humor of early American culture.

books.title: The War of the Worlds
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: The first-person narrative of the Martian invasion of 19th-century Earth.

books.title: Jeeves Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of short stories featuring Jeeves and Wooster and the upperclass English life of the early 1900s.

books.title: The Varieties of Religious Experience
books.author: William James
books.summary: A philospher and psychologist surveys direct religious experiences, including healthy-mindedness, saintliness, conversion and mysticism.

books.title: With the Empress Dowager of China
books.author: Katharine A. Carl
books.summary: A female painter becomes the court artist of the Empress Dowager of China.
